comments: Tommy - "I’m going to play you a track by somebody who, I don’t think, you’d expect to hear on a rock show. But I wanna play this track because I have great admiration for the guy. I spent an hour in his company the other day doing an overseas programme, he was an absolute gentleman, I think he’s a fine singer, and I really just back the guy up for the stance that he takes, but I’m not gonna tell you who it is till afterwards…"

Technical note: This seems to be a very good recording from a radio with poor reception! There is a bass hum throughout, hiss and crackles but it is mostly surprisingly listenable, especially during the louder songs. The quieter ones need a bit of tolerance…There are two minor dropouts: one between tracks 7 and 8 (Cockney Rejects and Girlschool). The back announcement makes it clear nothing is missing. The second towards the end of the Cliff Richard track.There is one major fault when a higher pitched whine turns up in tracks 9 and 10 (Chinatown and Billy Squier) rendering them virtually unlistenable.

track_listing.track: 'Burning Down One Side' (LP-Pictures At Eleven) Swan Song
'The Way Of The Rocker' (LP-The Wild Ones) AKA
'It Keeps You Rocking' (LP-Emotions In Motion) Capitol
'Time Is On My Side (live)' (Still Life) Rolling Stones
'Every Man Should Know (Queenie) (LP-Highway Song Live) Atco
'Thief In The Night' (LP-Now You See Me, Now You Don't) EMI
'Days Of Wine And Roses' (7" single) Polydor
